key_insight: An additive MLP-GNN model can separate physicochemical and structural drivers of aqueous solubility, making predictions more interpretable without sacrificing performance.
clinical_relevance: Improved solubility prediction can help prioritize drug-like compounds earlier in discovery by flagging candidates more likely to have developable formulations.
method: Encode physicochemical descriptors with an MLP (chemical branch).
Encode molecular graphs with a GNN (structural branch).
Combine branch outputs additively at prediction time, with an optional interaction term, and interpret each branch separately.
evidence_signal: Pretraining on AqSolDB and fine-tuning on BigSolDB2 improved accuracy and reduced run-to-run variability, with competitive performance across both datasets.
watch_item: Interpretability depends on the quality and domain coverage of the training data, so the learned chemical and structural attributions may not transfer cleanly to novel chemistries.
paper_title: An Additive MLP-GNN Framework for Characterizing Chemical and Structural Contributions to Aqueous Solubility
